methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $150M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $1.5B.

data.narrative:
Ares Industries is a Series A-stage Defense & Security company founded by Alex Tseng and Devan Plantamura, currently valued at $150M post-money and having raised $31M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 peers—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms in the same sector and adjacent stages, including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis, anchoring to the $150M last post-money valuation and applying a typical 2.0x Series A-to-next-stage step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $300M. Peer-Median analysis across 9 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a peer range spanning $60M to $1.5B. The median peer valuation reflects larger Series B-stage companies in the cohort.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $255M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median point of $650M. The wide spread reflects material differences between stage-progression (more conservative, 2.0x uplift) and peer-median (reflecting actual Series B valuations in the market). Key uncertainties center on the applicability of Series B comparables to a Series A company and variation in revenue scale, traction, and defensibility across the peer set.
